6513	\begin{lemma}
6514	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
6515	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
6516	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
6517	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
6518	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
6519	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
6520	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
6521	  % L180--199).
6522	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
6523	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
6524	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
6525	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
6526	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
6527	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
6528	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
6529	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
6530	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
6531	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
6532	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
6533	  \[
6534	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
6535	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
6536	  \]
6537	\end{lemma}
6538	
6539	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
6540	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
6541	% the sheaf
6542	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
6543	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
6544	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
6545	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
6546	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
6547	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
6548	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
6549	% opens we win."
6550	\begin{proof}
6551	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
6552	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
6553	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
6554	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
6555	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
6556	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
6557	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
6558	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
6559	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
6560	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
6561	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
6562	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
6563	
6564	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
6565	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
6566	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
6567	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
6568	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
6569	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
6570	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
6571	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
6572	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
6573	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
6574	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
6575	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
6576	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
6577	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
6578	  \(k \geq 1\).
6579	\end{proof}

6581	\begin{lemma}
6582	
6583	\leanok
6584	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]

6589	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when

6593	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).
6594	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.

6596	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
6597	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.

6599	  % $$

6612	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE
6613	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
6614	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
6615	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
6616	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
6617	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
6618	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
6619	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
6620	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
6621	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
6622	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
6623	  \[
6624	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
6625	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
6626	  \]

6628	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
6629	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
6630	
6631	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
6632	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
6633	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
6634	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
6635	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in
6636	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\), and it compares the (always-defined) {\v C}ech higher
6637	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)
6638	  against the derived-functor one only in that case. Second, we assert the comparison in the
6639	  weak form of the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism, i.e.\ as

6643	  relative Picard functor, local triviality of the comparison) require: they use

6645	  \(R^i f_*\) agree as objects, never a specific identification, so committing to a
6646	  canonical natural transformation would add coherence obligations that play no
6647	  role in the applications.

6658	  \begin{enumerate}

6660	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is

6671	  \[

6673	      \;\cong\;
6674	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).
6675	  \]

## Chapter 3: Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex (the monster chapter)

This chapter covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean plus ten other files. The key items I need to focus on are `lem:tile_section_comparison`, `lem:tile_section_localization`, and the chain of lemmas feeding into them.

Looking at `lem:tile_section_comparison` at line 4409, it's missing the `\lean{}` hint, but the proof sketch is thorough and complete.
